name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment globally under iconic brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. The company operates through Agriculture, Construction, and Financial Services segments. Full-year 2025 revenue was $18.1B (down 9% YoY) as the agricultural equipment industry navigated a historic demand downturn, though Q4 2025 showed 6% revenue growth and 21% industrial adjusted EBIT improvement. CEO Gerrit Marx (appointed July 2024) is executing a 'Breaking New Ground' strategy combining AI, autonomy, and precision technology with legacy equipment manufacturing.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Innovation Track Record: 2/3 — CNH has launched over 15 new tractor models, 10 harvesting platforms, and 30+ precision technology releases planned through 2027, representing consistent product innovation across its agricultural portfolio [1]. The 2022 Iveco Group spinoff strategically simplified the company into a pure-play ag/construction platform. AI Deployment: 2/2 — CNH's 2025 Tech Day showcased an 'AI Tech Assistant' deployed to hundreds of agricultural and construction dealer groups for real-time diagnostic support, and every member of the global leadership team is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program [1]. Market Share: 1/2 — CNH is a meaningful global player alongside Deere and AGCO in agricultural equipment but is not the category leader. Pipeline: 1/2 — specific next-gen precision agriculture products named with launch dates through 2027, though revenue visibility remains vague. R&D Intensity: 0/1 — R&D spending is slightly below Deere's intensity. Total: 2+2+1+1+0 = 6.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor: 3/4 — CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to sustainable food production and feeding a growing world. CEO Gerrit Marx has framed this explicitly, stating 'tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [1]. This is specific, thoughtful, and differentiates CNH from generic industrial companies. Community Impact: 0/2 — corporate philanthropy exists through employee foundations but is not distinctive in scale or specificity. Employee Retention: 1/2 — leadership reorganization under Marx in 2024-2025 was orderly with stable core teams; lean and agile organizational structure emphasizes clear accountability [2]. Insider Buying: 1/2 — minimal insider transaction data; Marx's appointment as CEO reflects board confidence but no documented net insider buying in the last 12 months. Total: 3+0+1+1 = 5.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Infrastructure calculated mechanically as a Growth stock (yield 0.92% below 2%). D/E of 333.7% scores 0.0 (above 300% floor, elevated leverage from Financial Services segment is structural for equipment companies but breaches the scoring threshold). ROE of 4.8% scores 0.32 (2x weight, well below 15% target as 2025 net income fell to $510M) [1]. Revenue growth YoY of -0.1% scores 0.66. Revenue growth 5yr CAGR of -8.4% scores 0.0 (2x weight, below -5% floor). Forward P/E of 14.9 scores 1.0. Profit margin of 2.1% scores 0.11. Forward PEG of 0.63 scores 1.0 (2x weight, strong value signal reflecting expected earnings recovery). Weighted sum: 4.42/10. Infrastructure = round(4.42) = 4. Liquidity: $10.85 x 15.8M = $171.8M [2].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection: 6/9 — CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment (tractors, harvesters, construction machinery) that cannot be replaced by AI software [1]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products with significant aftermarket services, dealer networks, and a captive financing arm, all of which are protected from AI substitution. However, competitors like Deere are investing heavily in autonomous farming equipment and AI-powered crop management, creating competitive pressure. AI Opportunity: 1/1 — CNH's AI Tech Assistant is deployed to hundreds of dealer groups for real-time diagnostic support, and the company's precision agriculture technology stack integrates AI for crop management and field analytics, representing tangible AI-driven value creation [1]. Total: 6+1 = 7.

infinite_game.summary: CNH has a genuine long-term purpose around sustainable agriculture and food security that shapes R&D investment and product strategy. CEO Marx's framing of AI as a 'generational shift' in farming productivity reflects infinite game thinking. The Iveco spinoff demonstrated existential flexibility. However, the 2025-2026 downturn response has been cautious, and the Iveco subsidiary's EU antitrust investigation raises governance questions.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably. CEO Marx has framed this explicitly, and the company has maintained R&D investment in 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 despite a 9% revenue decline [1]. This is a genuine cause, partially lived through sustained investment even in a downturn. Display: 7.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership changes in 2024-2025 under CEO Gerrit Marx. The lean and agile organizational structure emphasizes clear accountability across three Global Business Units [2]. 20% of leadership variable compensation is linked to sustainability goals, aligning incentives with long-term objectives. Orderly transition with stable core teams. Display: 6.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar and frames its product roadmap in terms of capability gaps to close. Management language focuses on delivering better products and improving margins rather than competitive destruction. Display: 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The 2022 Iveco Group spinoff was a transformative strategic decision that simplified CNH into a pure-play agriculture/construction company [1]. The shift from pure equipment manufacturer to tech-integrated precision agriculture platform represents meaningful but incremental strategic evolution. Display: 6.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades, CNH maintained investment in 15+ new tractor launches and 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 [1]. CEO Marx's commitment to 'moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects courageous investment through the cycle. However, Q1 2026 near-break-even guidance suggests some defensive posture. Display: 6.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality agricultural equipment franchise navigating a cyclical trough with visible recovery on the horizon. Q4 2025 showed 6% revenue growth and 21% YoY industrial adjusted EBIT improvement, beating EPS estimates by 73% at $0.19 vs $0.11 expected [1]. The forward PEG of 0.63 reflects strong expected earnings recovery, and the forward P/E of 14.9x values CNH at a significant discount to its historical trading range. The company's precision agriculture technology stack with 30+ releases through 2027 and every global leader running AI pilots positions it to capture greater software/service revenue per unit as the cycle turns. Management expects agricultural demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer destocking completes.
thesis_continued: The near-term bear case remains severe: full-year 2025 revenue fell 9% to $18.1B, net income collapsed from $2.3B in 2023 to $510M, and management guides for flat to slightly declining industrial net sales in 2026 with global agricultural demand down another 5% [1]. Q1 2026 is expected near break-even. D/E of 334% limits balance sheet flexibility during the downturn. Tariff costs created 110 basis points of EBIT margin headwind in 2025 with expectations for worsening in 2026. The 3-year revenue CAGR of -8.4% reflects significant contraction from the 2023 peak, and the legacy Iveco EU antitrust investigation remains unresolved [3]. John Deere's more aggressive autonomous and electric equipment investments could leave CNH competitively behind in precision agriculture monetization.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as inventory destocking completes and farmer income stabilizes, with CNH well-positioned from continued R&D investment through the downturn [1]
Precision agriculture technology monetization through the AI Tech Assistant and integrated crop management platform driving software/service revenue above hardware margins
Margin recovery from operating leverage as high-horsepower equipment volumes recover from historic trough levels
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2026 as farmer capital expenditure remains depressed, delaying the recovery that underpins the forward PEG of 0.63 [1]
Tariff escalation on international supply chains across US, UK, Brazil, and India manufacturing facilities creating margin headwinds flagged as worsening in 2026
John Deere and AGCO accelerating autonomous and electric equipment rollouts that position competitors ahead of CNH in precision agriculture software monetization [3]
sources: CNH Industrial Q4 2025 Earnings Release and Full Year 2025 Results, February 2026; CNH Industrial 2025 Tech Day
CNH Industrial Senior Leadership Team and organizational structure, cnh.com; Comparably CNH Industrial culture profile, accessed June 2026
CNH Industrial ESG and Sustainability disclosures; EU antitrust investigation filings; Yahoo Finance CNH key statistics, accessed June 2026
